title: nasturtium cardamine
intro: Nasturtium cardamine, also known as watercress or wild mustard, is a flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a perennial plant that grows in moist environments and has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like iron and calcium, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Nasturtium cardamine can be consumed as a raw herb, used in teas, or added to salads and soups, making it a versatile ingredient in herbal preparations.
